perturbation.original_text: 12. This Contract shall be governed by Buyer country laws and its appropriate courts of Paris. It is expressly agreed that if any dispute should arise, the competent jurisdiction of Paris in France shall be the sole competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. Except as aforementioned, the receiving Party shall have no obligations or restrictions with respect to any Proprietary Information which that receiving Party can prove: DEFTA Purchasing department 2016 ANNEX 2 - Non Disclosure Agreement (a) has come into the public domain prior to, or after the disclosure thereof and in such case through no wrongful act of tha t receiving Party, or (b) is already known to the receiving Party, as evidenced by written documentation in the files of the receiving Party, or (c) has been lawfully received from a third party without restrictions or breach of this Agreement, or (d) has been or is published without violation of this Agreement, or (e) is independently developed in good faith by an employee or employees of that receiving Party who did not have access to t he Proprietary Information, or (f) is approved for release or use by written authorization of the disclosing Party. 12. This Contract shall be governed by Buyer country laws and its appropriate courts of Paris. It is expressly agreed that if any dispute should arise, the competent jurisdiction of Paris in France shall be the sole competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the jurisdiction clause to a section outlining exceptions to proprietary information obligations obscures its importance and makes it less discoverable, potentially leading a party to overlook the governing law and jurisdiction.
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 25 of EU Regulation No 1215/2012 addresses jurisdiction agreements, requiring them to be clear and unambiguous. Hiding the jurisdiction clause within exceptions to confidentiality blurs its clarity, making it difficult to enforce the intended jurisdiction.
perturbation.justification: The perturbation explanation states that moving the jurisdiction clause makes it less discoverable, potentially leading a party to overlook the governing law and jurisdiction. The Law Explanation states that Article 25 of EU Regulation No 1215/2012 addresses jurisdiction agreements, requiring them to be clear and unambiguous, and that hiding the jurisdiction clause within exceptions to confidentiality blurs its clarity. The original text has a jurisdiction clause in section 12. The changed text moves that same clause into section 6, where it is surrounded by text from a different agreement, and is a list of exceptions to proprietary information obligations. The altered location obscures the jurisdiction clause. The scraped law snippet mentions Article 25 but does not provide details. The structural flaw of placing the jurisdiction clause in an irrelevant section contradicts the clarity and unambiguous nature required of jurisdiction agreements, creating a legal contradiction.
